articles.title: UN urges Equatorial Guinea to halt plans to return US deportees to home countries
articles.description:
Human rights experts make rare public appeal as US deportees describe being held in ‘prison-like’ conditions
Human rights experts at the United Nations issued a rare public appeal to Equatorial Guinea, urging the West African country to halt its plans to return US deportees to their home countries where they face political violence, torture and death.
The statement, cosigned by a representative of the African Commission on Human and People’s rights, adds diplomatic pressure on Equatorial Guinea, one of the world’s most repressive regimes, to comply with international human rights standards and avoid refoulement, or the expulsion of people to countries where they face persecution.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Remains of second US soldier who went missing during military exercises in Morocco have been recovered
articles.description:
Mariyah Symone Collington and Kendrick Lamont Key Jr, who also died, had fallen off a cliff during an off-duty hike
The remains of the second US army soldier who went missing during military exercises in Morocco have been recovered, the army said on Wednesday, ending a multinational search operation that deployed air, naval and artificial intelligence assets.
The soldier was identified as Spc Mariyah Symone Collington of Taveres, Florida, the US Army Europe and Africa said in a statement. She was 19 years old.

articles.title: ‘Blatant disregard for rights’: concern grows over Gabon’s social media clampdown
articles.description:
Activists claim use of laws to curtail internet freedoms part of well-documented history of cracking down on dissent
When Gabon’s media regulator indefinitely suspended major social media platforms in February, citing security concerns during anti-government protests, it became the talk of town – literally.
Within weeks of the announcement, use of Virtual Private Networks (VPNs) to bypass the restrictions surged in the central African country. When gendarmerie began stopping young men at road checkpoints in the capital Libreville and other urban centres to confiscate mobile phones with VPNs installed or detain the owners, warnings spread by word of mouth. Activists and opposition members said their accounts were also suspended due to efforts of state officials.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 「権利の露骨な無視」：ガボンのソーシャルメディア取り締まりに懸念高まる

articles.title: Internal displacements caused by violence or conflict at record high in 2025
articles.description:
The 32.3m surpasses those caused by disasters for the first time, as 82.2m people displaced in total around world
The number of internal displacements triggered by conflict or violence around the world reached a record high in 2025, surpassing the number of disaster-driven internal displacements for the first time.
A report published by the Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre (IDMC) shows that by the end of 2025 there were 32.3m conflict-driven internal displacements. That is 60% higher than those recorded the previous year, and – for the first time since data collection began in 2008 – above displacements driven by natural disasters, which reached 29.9m in 2025.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Gaborone gold rush: how Botswana rose to the top of men’s sprinting
articles.description:
Country with a population of just 2.5m credits investment in young athletes for its rise but this progress is under threat
It was a fairytale ending to the World Athletics Relays in Gaborone. In the final strait, Collen Kebinatshipi surged past South Africa’s Zakithi Nene to win the men’s 4x400m relay for Botswana. The home crowd, a sea of light blue, went wild.
“It means so many things to us,” Letsile Tebogo, 22, the reigning 200m Olympic champion, who ran the second leg, told reporters afterwards. “Not just the team … but for the people that always cheer for us behind the TV. Now they had that experience to see first-hand how much effort, how much pressure, how much we give for them.”
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Brazil’s Atlantic forest records lowest deforestation in 40 years
articles.description:
Environmentalists hail decline but warn weakened laws could reverse gains
Brazil’s Atlantic forest, the country’s most threatened biome, last year recorded its lowest level of deforestation since monitoring began 40 years ago, a new report shows.
The forest is Brazil’s most populous biome, and home to 80% of the population and major cities such as Rio de Janeiro and São Paulo. In 2025 it recorded 8,658 hectares of deforestation, marking the first time it has fallen below 10,000 hectares since 1985.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Cartel corruption claims push US-Mexico relations to breaking point
articles.description:
With Mexico under pressure from Trump to tackle drug trafficking groups, analysts say ‘it’s the most tense situation since the 1980s’
Relations between Mexico and the United States are being pushed to breaking point amid accusations by Washington that Mexican officials have been “in bed for years” with drug traffickers, and reports of CIA agents freely operating south of the border.
“There are many who are betting on the defeat and failure of the Mexican government,” said Claudia Sheinbaum tersely on Wednesday, when asked about the allegations at a news conference. ”We want a good relationship with the United States government. What are our limits? The defence of sovereignty and respect for the Mexican people and their dignity.”
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Les Simpson: return of Québécois show spares viewers from ‘European French’
articles.description:
Beloved animated series will return for 36th season in the fall after telecoms giant Bell Media reaches deal with Disney
Fans of Les Simpson have a message for anyone who doubted the future of the beloved and long-running Québécois version of the animated satirical show: Mange de la crotte.
Les Simpson will return for its 36th season in the fall after telecoms giant Bell Media said it had reached an agreement with Disney for the rights to air and dub the show. The deal caps nearly a year of uncertainty surrounding the adaptation, which is beloved in Canada’s lone francophone province.

A suspect accused of murdering his rock singer friend in 1993 has been apprehended in Central America after spending more than 30 years successfully evading authorities.
According to the US Marshals Service, Richard Werstine, who was wanted in connection with the killing of Cold as Life vocalist Rodney Barger, was arrested in Panama.

articles.title: Trump urged to rule out ‘unlawful’ Cuba takeover and stop using Guantánamo Bay for migrant detention
articles.description:
Exclusive: In letter seen by the Guardian, 30 members of Congress warn US president’s Cuba military operation would worsen ‘mass suffering’

US politics live – latest updates

More than 30 members of Congress have urged Donald Trump’s top officials to end the use of the Guantánamo Bay naval base for immigrant detention and rule out any plans for military action on Cuba.
In the letter to the secretaries of defense, state and homeland security on Wednesday morning, reviewed by the Guardian, Democratic lawmakers led by Delia Ramirez, a representative from Illinois, linked a rise in migration from the island nation to the heightening US aggression on Cuba.

articles.titleJa: フォン・デア・ライエン氏、子供のソーシャルメディア利用開始時期の延期をEUに要請
articles.summary: フォン・デア・ライエン氏はEU首脳会議で、子供たちのオンライン保護対策について専門家委員会が7月までに具体的な方策を打ち出すと述べた。

articles.title: Zelensky's ex-chief of staff in court as Ukraine corruption probe escalates
articles.description: Andriy Yermak was named by Ukraine's two anti-corruption agencies as a suspect in a money-laundering scheme.
articles.titleJa: ゼレンスキー前首席補佐官が汚職捜査で出廷、ウクライナの汚職捜査が激化
articles.summary: ウクライナの反汚職機関2つが、ゼレンスキー大統領の元首席補佐官アンドリー・イェルマックを資金洗浄計画の容疑者として名指し、汚職捜査が激化している。

articles.title: Russia's shadow fleet ships defying PM's threat and entering UK waters
articles.description: BBC Verify analysis of ship-tracking data suggests "shadow fleet" vessels sailed into UK water despite the government threatening to board them.

articles.summary: BBC Verifyの分析によると、英国政府が乗り込みを警告していたにもかかわらず、「影の艦隊」の船舶が英国海域に侵入したことが示唆されている。

articles.title: Who is Alex Batty and how was he found?
articles.description: Alex Batty vanished in 2017 while on holiday - now he is telling the full story for the first time.
articles.titleJa: アレックス・バッティとは誰か、そしてどのようにして発見されたのか？
articles.summary: 2017年に休暇中に行方不明になったアレックス・バッティが、初めてその全容を語る。彼の過去と発見の経緯に焦点が当てられている。

articles.title: 'Britain at the heart of Europe': How Starmer's plans are going down in the EU
articles.description: Sir Keir Starmer pledged to rebuild the UK's relationship with Europe in a defiant speech on Monday.
articles.titleJa: 「欧州の中心にある英国」：スターマー氏の計画がEUでどう受け止められているか
articles.summary: キア・スターマー氏が、英国と欧州の関係再構築を誓う演説を行った。その計画がEU内でどのように評価されているかが報じられている。

articles.title: Why Eurovision's fallout over Israel may change the competition forever
articles.description: With Eurovision facing its biggest boycott in 70 years, questions are growing over the competition's future.
articles.titleJa: イスラエルを巡るユーロビジョンでの余波が、コンテストを永遠に変える可能性
articles.summary: 過去70年間で最大のボイコットに直面しているユーロビジョンについて、その将来を巡る疑問が高まっている。イスラエルを巡る騒動が大会に与える影響を分析。

articles.title: Mexico cancels plans to end school year early for World Cup
articles.description: Parents were angry at a proposal to end classes on 5 June instead of 15 July due to the tournament and expected extreme heat.
articles.titleJa: メキシコ、ワールドカップのための早期休校計画を中止
articles.summary: ワールドカップと猛暑を理由とした早期休校案に保護者が反発し、メキシコ政府は計画を中止した。

articles.title: Trump ally Kari Lake tapped to be US ambassador to Jamaica
articles.description: Lake thanked President Trump for nominating her, saying on social media that Jamaica "is a country I know very well, full of incredible people".
articles.titleJa: トランプ氏の盟友カリ・レイク氏、駐ジャマイカ米大使に指名
articles.summary: トランプ前大統領の盟友であるカリ・レイク氏が、駐ジャマイカ米国大使に指名された。レイク氏はジャマイカについて「よく知っている国」と述べた。

articles.title: Tourist hotspot at 'end of the world' denies causing hantavirus outbreak
articles.description: The BBC reports from the city of Ushuaia, where experts have been sent to investigate the origins of the outbreak.
articles.titleJa: 「世界の果て」の観光地、ハンタウイルス発生源説を否定
articles.summary: アルゼンチンの観光地ウシュアイアが、ハンタウイルス発生源との報道を否定。専門家が感染源調査のため派遣された。

articles.title: BBC tests out England and Argentina's World Cup training grounds in Kansas City
articles.description: Will Grant visited one of the pitches where England captain Harry Kane and his team will call home ahead of the tournament.
articles.titleJa: BBCがカンザスシティにあるイングランドとアルゼンチンのワールドカップ練習場を試す
articles.summary: BBC記者が、ワールドカップを控えたイングランド代表とアルゼンチン代表が使用するカンザスシティの練習場を訪れ、その様子を伝えた。

articles.title: Advocaat, 78, set for World Cup record after Curacao return
articles.description: Dick Advocaat makes a surprise return as Curacao's head coach after his successor Fred Rutten resigned just a month out from their World Cup debut.

articles.title: Trump and Lula's private Oval Office meeting signals lingering strain - and effort to avoid tension
articles.description: The US and Brazilian presidents did not appear together publicly, but traded compliments after their talks.
articles.titleJa: トランプ氏とルーラ氏の私的な大統領執務室会談、残る緊張と衝突回避の努力を示唆
articles.summary: 米国とブラジルの大統領が非公開で会談。公の場での共演はなかったものの、会談後には互いに賛辞を送り、両国の関係における微妙な状況がうかがえた。

articles.title: How worried should we be about hantavirus?
articles.description: Passengers potentially exposed to hantavirus are being repatriated, so what is the risk to the wider public?
articles.titleJa: ハンタウイルスについて、どの程度懸念すべきか？
articles.summary: ハンタウイルスに感染した可能性のある乗客が送還されており、一般市民への感染リスクについて懸念が高まっている。

articles.title: How are countries responding to hantavirus?
articles.description: The UK, US and EU are asking all citizens returning home from the virus-hit MV Hondius to self-isolate for about six weeks.
articles.titleJa: 各国はハンタウイルスにどう対応しているか？
articles.summary: 英国、米国、EUは、ハンタウイルス発生船MVホンディウス号からの帰国市民に対し、約6週間の自己隔離を要請している。

articles.title: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
articles.description: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.
articles.titleJa: 世界の森林減少ペースは鈍化も、エルニーニョによる火災が脅威に
articles.summary: 熱帯雨林の減少ペースは昨年緩和されたが、エルニーニョ現象による火災が進行を脅かしており、依然として森林は急速に失われていると科学者は警鐘を鳴らす。

articles.title: The other life of US soldier accused of betting on Maduro's removal
articles.description: The Army officer charged with using classified information to win $400,000 is also a real estate investor with rave reviews on Airbnb.
articles.titleJa: マドゥロ氏失脚に賭けた米兵のもう一つの顔
articles.summary: 機密情報を使って40万ドルを稼いだ罪に問われている米陸軍将校が、Airbnbで高評価を得る不動産投資家という別の顔を持っていた。

articles.title: Who is defending Cuba’s threatened species?
articles.description: Cuba hosts some of the world’s richest biodiversity but faces the impacts of climate change, human activity and weak protections. Scientists and activists strive to preserve endangered wildlife and ecosystems.
articles.titleJa: キューバの絶滅危惧種を守るのは誰か？
articles.summary: キューバの豊かな生物多様性が気候変動や人間活動によって脅かされており、科学者や活動家が絶滅危惧種の保護に努めている。

articles.title: Brazil opens investigation on Google over its AI’s impact on the journalism industry
articles.description: Commissioner Diogo Thomson considered that the insertion of generative AI has “significantly altered the dynamics of access, visibility, and monetization of journalistic content in the digital environment.”
articles.titleJa: ブラジル、AIのジャーナリズム業界への影響を巡りGoogleへの調査を開始
articles.summary: ブラジル政府がGoogleの生成AIがジャーナリズムコンテンツのアクセス、可視性、収益化のダイナミクスを大きく変えたとして調査を開始した。

articles.title: The ordeal of an undocumented Haitian migrant in the Dominican Republic
articles.description: Regaining hope and confidence rests on finding a pathway to a country where he can regain his freedom and dignity, since persistent gang violence makes a return to Haiti impossible.
articles.titleJa: ドミニカ共和国におけるハイチ人不法移民の苦難
articles.summary: ドミニカ共和国にいるハイチ人不法移民が、故郷のギャング暴力により帰国できず、自由と尊厳を取り戻せる国を求めている苦難を描く。

articles.title: The human cost of the data center push in Latin America
articles.description: In this cross-border article, Global Voices is sharing stories from several countries in Latin America where communities are pushing back against AI-driven data center development.
articles.titleJa: ラテンアメリカにおけるデータセンター建設推進の人道的コスト
articles.summary: ラテンアメリカ各地で、AI関連のデータセンター建設が地域社会に与える人道的コストについて、複数の国の事例を交えながら報じる。

articles.title: Brazil: A warning on how AI and deepfakes can become an ’excessive risk‘ to women and girls
articles.description: A technical note by research center Internetlab points out that advances in synthetic image creators increase the risks of gender violence online and talks about platforms’ accountability
articles.titleJa: ブラジル：AIとディープフェイクが女性や少女にとって「過度なリスク」となる可能性への警告
articles.summary: ブラジルの調査機関が、AIによる合成画像技術の進歩がオンライン上でのジェンダー暴力のリスクを高め、プラットフォームの責任を指摘している。

articles.title: AI hype narrative reaches the public healthcare system in El Salvador
articles.description: This time we take you to El Salvador, examining government claims that an AI-supported transformation of the public healthcare system will make it the most advanced in the world.
articles.titleJa: AIブームの物語がエルサルバドルの公衆衛生システムに到達
articles.summary: エルサルバドル政府がAIを活用した公衆衛生システムの変革により、世界で最も先進的なシステムを構築すると主張していることについて検証する。

articles.title: The technological transformation of Colombia's armed conflict
articles.description: More than simply a uniform adoption of artificial intelligence, a technological innovation has emerged that is transforming the battlefield, decision-making processes, local narratives, and forms of social control.
articles.titleJa: コロンビアの武力紛争における技術的変革
articles.summary: コロンビアの武力紛争において、AIなどの技術革新が戦場、意思決定、地域の物語、社会統制の形を変革している現状を探る。

articles.title: When dry season never ends
articles.description: In an era marked by intense transnational mobility, the uneven distribution of rights and experiences in host communities reflects underlying dynamics of power and privilege that demand further scrutiny.
articles.titleJa: 乾季が終わらないとき
articles.summary: 国境を越えた人の移動が活発な時代に、受け入れコミュニティでの権利と経験の不均衡が、権力と特権の力学を反映しており、さらなる調査が求められる。

articles.title: How AI is used to influence global elections
articles.description: Companies that work in political persuasion are experimenting with AI at scale to see how they can make the traditional influencing methods even cheaper, faster, more convincing, and more automated.
articles.titleJa: AIはいかにして世界の選挙に影響を与えるか
articles.summary: 政治的説得に携わる企業が、AIを活用して従来の選挙介入手法をより安価、迅速、説得力があり自動化されたものにする方法を模索している。

articles.title: Community micro-hydropower plants revolutionise isolated areas of the Dominican Republic
articles.description: Community micro-hydropower projects in the DR rely on local leadership to deliver energy from renewable sources to isolated areas excluded from national distribution systems, contributing to reduced greenhouse gas emissions.
articles.titleJa: ドミニカ共和国の孤立地域を変革するコミュニティ型小水力発電所
articles.summary: ドミニカ共和国の孤立地域で、地域主導の小水力発電が再生可能エネルギーを供給し、温室効果ガス排出削減に貢献。国の配電網外の地域を変革する。

articles.title: A decade on, Trump returns to a stronger and more assertive China
articles.description: Beijing is arguably the most powerful competitor the US has confronted in its history, one analyst says.
articles.titleJa: 10年を経て、トランプはより強く、より自己主張の強い中国に戻る
articles.summary: トランプ氏の中国訪問と米中関係の現状について。中国が米国にとって最も強力な競争相手となっていることが指摘されている。

articles.title: Gunfire chaos as Philippine senator resists ICC arrest: What we know so far
articles.description: Gunshots rang out sparking chaos in the Senate building where Senator Ronald "Bato" dela Rosa had sought refuge.
articles.titleJa: フィリピンの上院議員がICCの逮捕に抵抗し銃撃で混乱：これまでにわかっていること
articles.summary: フィリピン上院議員が国際刑事裁判所(ICC)の逮捕に抵抗し、議事堂内で銃撃が発生、混乱が生じた事件の現状を報じる。

articles.title: Australia has some of the world's costliest homes. Will scrapping tax breaks help?
articles.description: The government hopes reforms will help young people enter the market, while critics say it will stifle supply.
articles.titleJa: オーストラリアは世界で最も住宅価格が高い国の一つ。税優遇措置の廃止は役立つか？
articles.summary: オーストラリアの住宅価格高騰に対し、政府が税優遇措置の廃止を検討。若者の住宅市場参入を促す狙いだが、供給抑制を懸念する声も。

articles.title: Alleged scammer extradited after  hacking attempt on BTS star Jungkook
articles.description: The suspect allegedly tried to transfer millions of dollars worth of shares from the K-pop singer's account.
articles.titleJa: BTSジョングクのハッキング未遂後、詐欺容疑者が身柄引き渡し
articles.summary: BTSのジョングクの口座から数百万ドル相当の株式を不正送金しようとした詐欺容疑者が、身柄を引き渡された。

articles.title: Indigenous Australians win record A$150m after billionaire mined without permission
articles.description: The Yindjibarndi people had sought A$1.8bn compensation for cultural damage and economic loss.
articles.titleJa: オーストラリア先住民、億万長者が無許可採掘後に過去最高の1億5000万豪ドルを獲得
articles.summary: オーストラリアの先住民Yindjibarndiの人々が、億万長者による無許可採掘に対し、文化的な損害と経済的損失の賠償として過去最高の1億5000万豪ドルを獲得。

articles.title: BJP leader back to head India's Assam state for second time in a row
articles.description: Himanta Biswa Sarma is seen as one of the architects of BJP's rapid growth in India's northeast.
articles.titleJa: BJP指導者、インドのアッサム州首相に2期連続で再任
articles.summary: ヒマンタ・ビスワ・サルマ氏がインドのアッサム州首相に2期連続で再任。同氏はインド北東部におけるBJPの急速な成長の立役者と見られている。

articles.title: Ex-Philippine leader Duterte's drug war enforcer escapes ICC arrest
articles.description: Senator Ronald "Bato" Dela Rosa, a former national police chief, has sought refuge in the senate.
articles.titleJa: フィリピン元大統領ドゥテルテ氏の麻薬戦争執行者がICCの逮捕を逃れる
articles.summary: フィリピンの元警察長官であるロナウド・“バト”・デラ・ロサ上院議員が、国際刑事裁判所(ICC)による逮捕を逃れ、上院で保護を求めた。

articles.title: Elon Musk and Jensen Huang among CEOs joining Trump on China trip
articles.description: More than a dozen US executives have joined the president on his visit, where he will meet his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping.

articles.title: Police find body believed to be of fugitive Australian shooter
articles.description: A body found in New South Wales is thought to be that of a man wanted over a triple murder.
articles.titleJa: 警察、逃亡中のオーストラリア人銃撃犯とみられる遺体を発見
articles.summary: ニューサウスウェールズ州で発見された遺体が、三件の殺人容疑で指名手配されていた男のものとみられている。警察が捜査を継続中。

articles.title: Pakistan struck a rehab centre and killed 269 Afghans. Their families want to know why
articles.description: There are calls to investigate the 16 March airstrike - which the UN says likely killed more - as a war crime.
articles.titleJa: パキスタンがリハビリ施設を攻撃しアフガン人269人が死亡。遺族は理由を知りたいと訴え
articles.summary: パキスタンによるリハビリ施設への空爆でアフガン人269人が死亡。国連は死者がさらに多いと指摘し、戦争犯罪として調査を求める声が高まっている。

articles.title: Members advance transparency work in rules of origin, explore trade facilitation links
articles.description: At a meeting of the Committee on Rules of Origin (CRO) on 11-12 May, WTO members advanced work on transparency and notification practices, discussed the future direction of work on preferential rules of origin for least-developed countries (LDCs), and participated in an information session on the links between trade facilitation and rules of origin. The meeting was chaired by Ms Carol Tsang of Hong Kong, China.
articles.titleJa: メンバーは原産地規則の透明性作業を進め、貿易円滑化との関連を探る
articles.summary: WTO原産地規則委員会が会合を開き、透明性向上のための作業を進め、途上国向け特恵原産地規則の将来の方向性や貿易円滑化との関連について議論した。

articles.title: Sweden contributes SEK 2 million to WTO capacity-building activities
articles.description: Sweden has given SEK 2 million (just under CHF 170,000) to the WTO's Global Trust Fund for 2026 to finance technical assistance and training programmes for government officials from developing economies and least-developed countries (LDCs). The contribution was confirmed at a signing ceremony on 8 May at the WTO attended by Ambassador Nina Tornberg of Sweden and the WTO's Director of the Finance and Corporate Services Division, Andrew Rizk.
articles.titleJa: スウェーデンがWTOの能力構築活動に200万スウェーデンクローナを拠出
articles.summary: スウェーデンは、途上国および後発開発途上国の政府職員を対象としたWTOの技術支援および研修プログラムに、200万スウェーデンクローナ（約17万スイスフラン）を拠出した。

articles.title: General Council chair outlines next steps to build on momentum from MC14 negotiations
articles.description: At the first meeting of the General Council since the 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14), the newly elected Chairperson, Ambassador Clare Kelly (New Zealand), reported on her consultations with members on how to continue discussions in Geneva to build upon the high level of convergence on several areas of work at MC14 held in Yaoundé, Cameroon. Members also took note of Türkiye's announcement that it was joining the growing convergence to extend the e-commerce moratorium, with many welcoming the additional progress in narrowing gaps in the discussions.
articles.titleJa: 一般理事会議長、MC14交渉の勢いを活かす次なるステップを概説
articles.summary: WTO一般理事会がMC14後初の会合を開催。議長は今後の議論の進め方について報告し、電子商取引モラトリアム延長に関するトルコの動きも歓迎された。

articles.title: United States notifies import surcharges, ready for WTO consultations
articles.description: At a meeting of the Committee on Balance-of-Payments Restrictions held on 5 May, WTO members discussed the United States’ new notification regarding the imposition of import surcharges to address the country’s serious balance-of-payments deficits. The United States said the surcharges took effect on 24 February 2026 and will expire on 24 July 2026, unless extended by an act of Congress. Members welcomed the transparency of the United States and its readiness to engage in consultations in line with WTO rules.
articles.titleJa: 米国、輸入課徴金を通知しWTO協議の準備を表明
articles.summary: 米国は、深刻な国際収支赤字に対処するため、輸入課徴金を課す新たな通知を行った。WTO加盟国は米国の透明性と協議への意欲を歓迎した。

articles.title: WTO members discuss action plan for cotton sector after MC14, World Cotton Day 2026
articles.description: WTO members reviewed global cotton market trends and the path ahead for the cotton sector at meetings in Geneva on 4 May. The meetings follow the high-level cotton event at the 14th WTO Ministerial Conference (MC14) in Cameroon in March. Delegations noted commitments announced at MC14 to invest in African countries moving up the cotton value chain and stressed the vital contribution of cotton to international trade and its potential to foster sustainable development.
articles.titleJa: WTO加盟国、MC14と2026年世界綿花デー後に綿花セクターの行動計画を議論
articles.summary: WTO加盟国はジュネーブで会合を開き、世界の綿花市場の動向と今後の行動計画を議論。MC14で発表されたアフリカ諸国への投資コミットメントが強調された。

articles.title: Trade Policy Review: Belize
articles.description: The fourth review of the trade policies and practices of Belize takes place on 4 and 6 May 2026. The basis for the review is a report by the WTO Secretariat and a report by the Government of Belize.
articles.titleJa: 貿易政策レビュー：ベリーズ
articles.summary: 2026年5月4日と6日に、ベリーズの貿易政策と慣行に関する第4回レビューが開催される。WTO事務局とベリーズ政府の報告書が審査の基礎となる。

articles.title: Members adopt rules of procedure at first Fisheries Subsidies Committee meeting
articles.description: WTO members held the first regular meeting of the Committee on Fisheries Subsidies on 1 May 2026, marking a milestone in the implementation of the Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies. They approved various decisions on the functioning of the Committee.
articles.titleJa: 加盟国、漁業補助金委員会初回会合で議事規則を採択
articles.summary: WTO漁業補助金委員会が初の定例会合を開催し、漁業補助金協定の実施における重要な節目となった。委員会運営に関する複数の決定が承認された。

articles.title: Transparency on subsidies remains central focus of SCM Committee
articles.description: At regular and special meetings of the Committee on Subsidies and Countervailing Measures (SCM) on 30 April 2026, improving transparency through the timely and complete submission of subsidy notifications was once again a central theme of the Committee’s work. The Chair, Jungsoo Hur of the Republic of Korea, emphasized the critical importance of transparency for the effective operation of the Committee and expressed concern about the continued low level of compliance with notification obligations. He strongly encouraged members to submit their outstanding notifications as soon as possible.
articles.titleJa: 補助金に関する透明性がSCM委員会の中心的な焦点であり続ける
articles.summary: 補助金及び相殺措置委員会は、補助金通知の適時かつ完全な提出による透明性向上に注力。議長は遵守状況の低さに懸念を表明し、速やかな通知提出を促した。

articles.title: WTO opens online registration for 2026 Public Forum, launches call for proposals
articles.description: The WTO has opened online registration for this year’s Public Forum, titled Powering the Future, which will take place at the WTO premises in Geneva from 15 to 17 September. It has also launched a call for proposals for the event, which this year will focus on services trade.
articles.titleJa: WTO、2026年公共フォーラムのオンライン登録を開始し、提案を募集
articles.summary: WTOは、今年の公共フォーラム「未来を動かす」のオンライン登録を開始し、提案を募集中。フォーラムは9月15日から17日にジュネーブで開催され、サービス貿易に焦点を当てる。

articles.title: Canada commits CAD 500,000 to strengthen food safety and plant and animal health capacity
articles.description: Canada is providing CAD 500,000 (approximately CHF 286,000) to the Standards and Trade Development Facility (STDF) to strengthen sanitary and phytosanitary (SPS) capacity of public and private sector stakeholders in developing and least-developed countries. The contribution will support STDF activities that help countries meet international food safety and plant and animal health standards and facilitate safe trade, based on the STDF Strategy for 2025-2030.
articles.titleJa: カナダ、食品安全と植物・動物衛生能力強化のため50万カナダドルを拠出
articles.summary: カナダは、開発途上国および後発開発途上国の食品安全と植物・動物衛生能力強化のため、STDFに50万カナダドルを拠出。これにより、国際基準達成と安全な貿易促進を支援する。
